url.description:
A URL or URL template specifying where the results shall be accessed. Supported formats:
  • a valid Amazon S3 URL, e.g. `s3://my-personal-bucket/some-folder`. A subdirectory will be created for this async process request, named after the request ID, and output files will be stored there. This is equivalent to the template `s3://my-personal-bucket/some-folder//`.
  • a templated Amazon S3 URL, e.g. `s3://my-personal-bucket/some-folder/`. Templating allows custom organization of the output files. Templates can contain the following placeholders, which are replaced by respective actual values when writing results:
    • `` - the ID of your async process request,
    • `` - the output of the async request.
    If the request is not templated `/` will be used as default.

iamRoleARN.description: IAM role ARN, which allows programmatic access to the S3 bucket specified in the `url` field using the recommended assume IAM role flow.

accessKey.description: AWS access key, which allows programmatic access to the S3 bucket specified in the `url` field.

secretAccessKey.description: AWS secret access key which must correspond to the AWS access key.

region.description: The region where the S3 bucket is located. If omitted, the region of the Sentinel Hub deployment that the request is submitted to is assumed.
